summary: The sendPriceAlert catch block catches DB errors from the success path, so a successful email send with a DB logging failure would be incorrectly recorded as 'failed'. Additionally, user-controlled strings (game titles, store names) are interpolated into HTML templates without escaping, which can break email rendering.
keyFindings: Logic error in sendPriceAlert: the try/catch wraps both the resend.emails.send() call (line 80) and the db.insert() success-logging call (line 92). If the email sends successfully but the DB insert on line 92 throws (e.g. connection error, constraint violation), execution falls into the catch block which records emailStatus: 'failed' — even though the email was actually sent. The success-path DB insert should be separated from the email send try/catch, or the catch should distinguish between send errors and DB errors.

HTML injection risk: gameTitle is interpolated unescaped into an HTML alt attribute (alt="${gameTitle}") and into element text content. A game title containing double quotes or HTML special characters (e.g. 'Game <Special> Edition' or 'Tom & Jerry') will break the alt attribute or produce malformed HTML. storeName and price have the same issue. All user-sourced strings should be HTML-escaped before interpolation.
